title: Update working memory from folder manual ordering session
summary: Durable context from the session that added persisted manual ordering for folders and folder conversations, including the accepted recency-ordering trade-off and one unresolved Timeline storage-access report.

claims.text: The shared folder model persists manual ordering with optional `sortIndex` fields on both folders and folder conversation references.

Folder sidebar ordering keeps pinned folders ahead of unpinned folders and starred conversations ahead of normal conversations; within each pinned or starred group, items with `sortIndex` are ordered by that value before falling back to the previous name or time sort.

FolderManager data integrity now fills missing `sortIndex` values: folders are initialized from the existing name-based sibling order, and conversations are initialized from the existing `lastOpenedAt`/`addedAt` time order.

Conversation reordering is handled directly on conversation elements with top/bottom half detection and `gv-reorder-above`/`gv-reorder-below` indicators; folder reordering uses `gv-reorder-gap` drop zones between folder items.

The manual-ordering implementation can reorder folders within a parent or move them to another parent at a target index, and can reorder or move one or more conversations while adjusting same-folder insertion indexes after removal and clamping the final target index.

The accepted behavior is full manual conversation ordering after migration: once `ensureSortIndices()` has populated `sortIndex`, `lastOpenedAt` is only the initial-order input and later clicks should not move conversations ahead of the user's manual order.

The session rejected the alternative where `sortIndex` would be written only after a user drags an item and un-dragged conversations would continue to sort by `lastOpenedAt`; the user approved keeping the current fully manual ordering model.

Manual reordering must preserve the existing folder drop semantics: dropping onto a folder moves the item into that folder, while dropping between folders reorders and dropping a conversation onto another conversation's upper or lower half reorders relative to that conversation.

Investigate whether TimelineManager.reapplyPosition should suppress or more gracefully handle `Extension context invalidated` storage-access failures; the issue was reported during the folder-ordering session but was not resolved in that slice.

metadata.reason: The user asked whether last-opened sorting had effectively been canceled; the accepted response explained that populated `sortIndex` makes `lastOpenedAt` only an initial migration input so clicks do not disturb manual order, and the user approved that behavior.

The assistant offered the alternative of not auto-populating `sortIndex` so un-dragged conversations would keep `lastOpenedAt` ordering; the user answered that the current behavior was good and asked to commit it.

The session distinguished dropping onto a folder from dropping between items, then refined conversation reordering so conversation-on-conversation drops use upper/lower position while folder-on-folder behavior remains a move.

The user reported `[Timeline] reapplyPosition storage access failed: Error: Extension context invalidated.` in the same message as the conversation drag issue; the accepted follow-up fixed drag behavior and did not address this Timeline report.
